global.title: Feed count scales linearly with drives and documents; op-feed reads fan out across N-1 peers
global.content:
# Feed and Upload Counts

## Single-user totals (for 1 drive + 2 docs)

- 1 user manifest feed
- 1 drive manifest feed (per drive)
- 1 document manifest feed (per document)
- Total: `1 + 1 + 2 = 4 feeds`

## /bytes uploads

- 1 per feed index update (the manifest JSON)
- 1 per operation batch
- Example: `4 manifest uploads + 2 op batch uploads = 6 /bytes refs`

## Collaboration scaling (N users)

- Each user writes to exactly 1 op feed per collaborative document
- Each user reads N-1 op feeds per collaborative document
- With GSOC/PSS push notifications, reads happen only on actual edits — bandwidth scales with edit frequency, not user count
- Without push, every reader polls every peer feed — O(N) polling load

## Design consequence

Feed count grows linearly with documents, not sub-linearly — there is no "one big feed" compression. This is acceptable because feed creation is cheap; the expensive operation is `/bytes` uploads (stamp cost).
global.description: A user's feed count is 1 user manifest + 1 per drive + 1 per document; for N collaborators each reads N-1 op feeds and writes to 1, so read fanout grows with participants.
